Storm Delia leaves 120 homes without power in County Antrim

Updated 14:02, 14 July 2026

Storm Delia swept across the north coast overnight, leaving 45 homes without power and closing several rural roads, the electricity network operator said on Monday morning.

Engineers were dispatched at first light and expect the majority of connections to be restored before evening, although some outlying properties may remain cut off until Tuesday.

"We have never seen July winds like this in thirty years of records," said operations director Maire Connolly, urging residents to report fallen lines rather than approach them.

The Met Office had issued an amber warning on Sunday evening, and local councils opened two emergency shelters which a spokesperson described as a precaution that in the end only a handful of families used.

Ferry sailings between Ballycastle and Rathlin Island were suspended for the day, and organisers of the Lammas Fair said they would review arrangements for the coming weekend before making any decision on cancellation.
